title: Crispy Sage Norwegian Salmon Grill
description: A festive Norwegian salmon dish with a crisp sage-infused glaze, grilled to perfection for a satisfying, kosher-friendly dinner.

ingredients.name: Sea salt and cracked black pepper

instructions: Preheat a gas or charcoal grill to medium‑high (around 375–400 °F/190–200 °C).
Pat the salmon dry with paper towels and season both sides generously with sea salt and cracked pepper.
Whisk together the olive oil, melted butter, honey, Dijon mustard, minced garlic, and lemon zest to create the sage glaze.
Place the salmon fillets on the preheated grill, skin side down, and cook for 4–5 minutes until the skin is crisp and the fillets easily release from the grates.
Flip the fillets and brush the glaze over the flesh; grill for another 3–4 minutes, basting every minute, until the internal temperature reaches 125 °F (52 °C).
Optional: Finish the fillets under the broiler for 1–2 minutes to caramelize the glaze and deepen the crispness.
Remove the salmon from the grill, let rest 2 minutes, then drizzle with remaining glaze and serve immediately with a wedge of lemon and a sprinkle of fresh sage.
